nodes.context: South African prison gang divided into 26s, 27s, and 28s factions.
Faction of Numbers Gang responsible for smuggling and wealth accumulation.
Faction of Numbers Gang acting as judiciary and enforcement division.
Faction of Numbers Gang responsible for fighting and protection.
South African criminal organization present in most South African prisons, divided into 26s, 27s and 28s.
A group within the Numbers Gang, declared 'ruling party' in Belville and Brandvlei prison wars.
A group within the Numbers Gang.
A group within the Numbers Gang, traditionally dominant in Western Cape prisons.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang is divided into groups — the 26s, 27s and 28s.
edges.context: The 26s are a sub-group of the Numbers Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang is divided into groups — the 26s, 27s and 28s.
edges.context: The 27s are a sub-group of the Numbers Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang is divided into groups — the 26s, 27s and 28s.
edges.context: The 28s are a sub-group of the Numbers Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The 27s operate as the justice system which exists within the Number. Their primary historical purpose was to act as a buffer during conflicts between the 26s and 28s.
edges.context: The 27s mediate conflicts between the 26s and 28s.

edges.evidence_quote: The two 28s are not allowed to speak directly to the 26s. They communicate through the 27s.
edges.context: The 26s and 28s have a structured separation, communicating only through the 27s.

edges.evidence_quote: The 27s operate as the justice system which exists within the Number. Their primary historical purpose was to act as a buffer during conflicts between the 26s and 28s.
edges.context: The 27s mediate conflicts between the 26s and 28s.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang is divided into groups — the 26s, 27s and 28s.
edges.context: The 26s are a sub-group of the Numbers Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang is divided into groups — the 26s, 27s and 28s.
edges.context: The 27s are a sub-group of the Numbers Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang is divided into groups — the 26s, 27s and 28s.
edges.context: The 28s are a sub-group of the Numbers Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: When one of the number gangs feels it can take control of the prison, they declare a 'general election' in which an all-out war is waged, often lasting up to two years, until one gang is declared the 'ruling party'.
edges.context: The 26s and 28s have fought wars for control of prisons, with the 26s declared 'ruling party' in Belville and Brandvlei.
